title: Level 200.8
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 200.8: "Marbleous Mines"}}
{{Blurbackground|file=Marbleous mines.jpg|opacity=0.5}}{{Marble Coasts Theme}}{{Audio|file=Green_to_blue.mp3|volume=1|loop=yes|align=center}}{{Class Custom Image|bg-image=Marbleous mines.jpg|bg-size=900px|text-color=rgb(210,220,210)|class=1e - Environmental|entity=Lifeless|hexagon=1E|safety=Varying Safety|security=Environmental Hazards}}
'''Level 200.8''' is a sublevel between '''[[Level 200]]''' and '''[[Level 201]]'''.

== Description ==
{{Blurimage|align=Right|file=Marbleous mines.jpg|caption=The quarry.}}
'''Level 200.8''', also known as '''"Marbleous Mines"''', is an expansive abandoned marble quarry carved into a mountain range. The streaked white stone appears to have been methodically chiseled into gradually elevating levels to aid vertical travel (or the extraction of large blocks from the upper sections). Traversing the upper ledges requires extreme caution, however, as the ever-present wind in the sublevel may fluctuate without warning with enough force to send wanderers plummeting off the edge and straight to the bottom.

In the years since its discovery, '''Level 200.8''' has become a sort of primitive message board for wanderers. Thousands of words, phrases, drawings and names have been painstakingly carved into the marble, creating a breathtaking, limitless mural of human expression. Quotes, encouragements, declarations of love or simply one's own name are among the most common. The messages are written in thousands of languages, from English, Spanish, and Latin to indecipherable symbols, the meaning of which have been lost to time. Some have even attempted to carve sculptures or passageways of their own out of the rock, a testament to creativity despite the sub-level's harsh conditions. Directions have even been carved in some places to aid escape, since '''Level 200.8''' is Euclidian and its structure does not change unless altered by wanderers.

There may occasionally be smaller, human-sized tunnels connected to the larger, open quarries. As a general rule, wanderers should never travel into spaces where they cannot see the sky. If one begins to notice a lack of messages on the walls or natural light, they should immediately turn around. Becoming lost in the smaller tunnels is a serious risk, though it can occasionally lead to an exit (see '''Exits'''). Unlike '''[[Level 200]], Level 200.8''' contains no auditory anomalies; sound appears to behave completely normally, aside from the natural echoes created by the scale of the sublevel's environment.

=== Outside ===
Throughout the inner quarry, light streams through massive gashes in the cavern walls and ceiling, leading to the outer region of the level. Here, the sky is bright and harsh, despite the apparent lack of any visible sun. There are no clouds nor water in any form on the surface- only steep geometric carvings, loose rubble and abandoned excavation equipment. All of the equipment seems to be out of fuel, but have been proven to work perfectly when provided with whatever power source they usually rely on. They are often used by the '''[[M.E.G.|MEG]]''' for heavier contraction projects, since there seems to be an endless supply of them.{{Blurimage|file=Marble mines2.jpg|caption=The outside.}}Despite strong winds of marble dust and even occasional sandstorms, the sharp angles of '''Level 200.8'''<nowiki/>'s quarries never seem to erode or smooth over. However, the rock can still be easily harvested or carved into by non-marble substances, though it does appear to have at least one anomalous property that only takes effect once it is transported outside of the sublevel (see '''Entrances''').

Vast mountains of marble are visible in every direction, endlessly, and the loose particles of marble dust in the air create a distant haze. The sky is blue and cloudless, since there is not enough water in the atmosphere to create a weather cycle. As such, dehydration is also somewhat of a risk in '''Level 200.8''', so bringing liquids such as '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]''' will aid exploration. No [[Object List|'''objects''']] appear naturally within the sublevel; wanderers must procure their own supplies beforehand.

Like '''[[Level 200]]''', '''Level 200.8'''<nowiki/>'s day/night cycle appears to be the same as that of the '''[[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]'''. At night, the sublevel is entrenched in complete darkness, despite the fact that there is no sun in the sky during the day; light in '''Level 200.8''' still behaves as if there is one, emitting from a specific direction from the sky and even changing to a different hue in the morning and evening. Attempting to travel during this time without a light source is heavily unadvised, since a single wrong step could send one plummeting off a cliff. Ideally, wanderers should seek shelter or escape before nightfall.

Even when on the surface of the sublevel, all wanderers and whatever technology they bring with them will be functionally isolated. There is no internet connection or native electrical infrastructure. No electrical signals are able to enter or escape the level, contrary to the common application of '''[[Phenomenon 2]]''' throughout the rest of the Backrooms. For example, cell phones work perfectly but without cell service; communication devices like walkie-talkies only function if the receiving end of the device is also somewhere in the sublevel. Of course, all technology will eventually die if kept in '''Level 200.8''' long enough due to the lack of any energy sources.
== Life ==
No organic life is naturally found within '''Level 200.8''', entities, wanderers or otherwise. Inhospitable conditions and lack of resources have discouraged major organizations from using '''Level 200.8''' as anything but an extensive marble quarry. No plants can take root in the harsh marble, underground or on the surface, and there is not enough water to sustain them regardless, so creating an ecosystem is impossible. Theoretically, the bodies of any wanderers who died here would never decompose, but none have been found. Some people theorize any corpses are somehow transported to the [[Waste Disposal|'''Waste Disposal''']].

Due to the scarcity of entrances and the sheer scale of '''Level 200.8''', it is unknown whether it is affected by some variant of [[Phenomenon 15|'''Phenomenon 15''']]; no wanderer has ever come face to face with another while in the sublevel. At the very least, the abundant wall carvings prove that all wanderers experience the same version of '''Level 200.8''', whether or not they can meet each other within it.

== Entrances & Exits ==

=== Entrances ===

* Attempting to carve anything into the walls of '''[[Level 200]]''' will lead here.
* Notably, standing too close to a statue formed from '''Level 200.8'''<nowiki/>'s marble in any other '''[[Level]]''' seems to have a chance to suddenly [[No-Clipping|'''noclip''']] wanderers here. This phenomenon is under investigation. Please exercise caution and restraint around said statues in [[M.E.G.|'''MEG''']] common areas.

=== Exits ===

* Organically shaped tunnels deep in the interior can lead to '''[[Level 8]]'''.
* Some wanderers reported waking up in '''[[Level 20]]''' after falling asleep in the seat of a construction vehicle.
* No-clipping into a perfect 90 degree angled wall on the surface will lead to '''[[Level 391]]'''.

== Discovery ==
It is unknown exactly when '''Level 200.8''' was first discovered, since every recorded encounter describes existing messages carved into its walls. It was officially added to the database in 2002. While not suitable for habitation, '''Level 200.8''' continues to be a sort of artistic retreat for the average wanderer, and a source of rare and expensive looking material for those with the resources to transport it (though the recent discovery of its anomalous properties has somewhat lessened demand).
